reforms.description: The New Zealand government created a contestable fund for research distributed across ten Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) to develop direct partnerships with businesses, ensuring a more demand-led approach to research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund promotes partnerships between research institutes and businesses, enhancing innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a contestable fund represents a significant structural change in how research is funded and conducted in partnership with the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Several innovations have occurred in government funding since 1991, involving the creation of a contestable fund for research distributed across ten Crown Research Institutes (CRIs) which each serve specific industry groups.

reforms.description: The government formed Crown Research Units to enhance research capabilities and foster innovation in various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing research capabilities, these units contribute to innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The units are operational and contributing to research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of Crown Research Units represents a major reform in the structure and focus of research in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The formation of Crown Research Units.
